Oracc: SAAo/SAA01 — The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West

SAA 01 247. Bringing Deportees into Cities (ABL 0585)[via saao/saa01]

Obverse
oo beginning broken away

(Beginning destroyed)

o 1'1'

[ša LUGAL be-lí] ⸢TAv⸣ LÚv.ḫu-⸢ub-ti*⸣

(1) [As to the ... whom the king my lord sent] to me with the captives, I have brought them [into the land of] the Chief Cupbearer and into [the cities of Ti]llê, Si'immê, Naṣibina and Isana; the exhausted ones I made enter Naṣibina on the 23rd.

o 2'2'

[ú-še-bil]-an-ni-ni

o 3'3'

[ina KUR—LÚv].GAL—KAŠ.LUL

[ina URU].⸢til⸣-le-e

[ina URU].si-ʾi-me-e

[ina] ⸢URU⸣.na-ṣi-bi-na

o 7'7'

[ina] URU.i-sa-na

b.e. 10'b.e. 10'

UD 23-KAM

b.e. 11'11'

ina URU.na-ṣib-na

r 1r 1

ú-se-ri-ib

r 22

⸢bir*⸣-ti* ⸢e*⸣-ni

(r 2) I impressed upon the deputies that it was a royal command that they should bring the people into fortified places where there is good water. [The]y said to me: "[......

Adapted from Simo Parpola, The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West (State Archives of Assyria, 1), 1987. Lemmatised by Mikko Luukko, 2009-11, as part of the AHRC-funded research project “Mechanisms of Communication in an Ancient Empire: The Correspondence between the King of Assyria and his Magnates in the 8th Century BC” (AH/F016581/1; University College London) directed by Karen Radner. The annotated edition is released under the Creative Commons Attribution Share-Alike license 3.0. Please cite this page as http://oracc.org/saao/P334403/.
